text.title: Value Stream Management Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules grounding VSM. Each rule pairs a binary check + empirically anchored rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Lead Time MUST be measured from "user filed request" to "user received value" — NOT from "ticket created" to "merged". Reports that use internal endpoints are rejected.

rationale.source: Mik Kersten — Project to Product (2018); Forsgren/Humble/Kim — Accelerate (2018)

rule.rationale: Internal pipeline metrics (commit-to-deploy) can look elite while the customer waits weeks for spec, design, and compliance stages that precede the first commit. Only customer-anchored boundaries reveal actual flow.

rule.statement: Flow data MUST be split by Kersten's four work types: feature, defect, risk, debt. Single-number flow metrics are rejected as misleading.

rationale.source: Kersten Flow Framework; DevOps Research and Assessment cohort studies

rule.rationale: A team shipping 100% defect work has high throughput but zero feature velocity; aggregating disguises this. Four-type split is the minimum granularity.

rule.statement: Never optimise one DORA metric in isolation. Reports MUST always show the full four-tuple (DF, CLT, CFR, MTTR) AND pair DF with CFR. Single-metric optimisation is rejected.

rationale.source: Accelerate (Forsgren/Humble/Kim); DORA State of DevOps reports 2019-2023

rule.rationale: Deployment frequency can be gamed by deploying trivial changes. AI productivity that lifts DF while raising CFR erases the gain. The four-tuple interacts.

rule.statement: A constraint MUST NOT be declared with < 50 items per work type in the analysis window. With insufficient data, emit `insufficient_data: true` and stop.

rationale.source: Little's Law statistical-validity research; DORA panel-size guidance

rule.rationale: Constraint identification flips month-to-month with low data volume. >= 50 items per type is the minimum sample for Little's Law to hold within reasonable noise.

rule.statement: The report MUST NOT publish a DORA tier label ("Elite", "High") without trend context (>= 2 quarters of data + delta vs prior period). Static-label reports are rejected.

rationale.source: Accelerate methodology — levels are improvement benchmarks, not badges; Goodhart's Law cohort observations

rule.rationale: Publishing static levels invites teams to optimise for the scorecard, destroying the signal. Trend context preserves the diagnostic value.

rule.statement: When AI-generated PRs are present in the dataset, the report MUST report `human_prs` and `bot_prs` counts separately for DF and Throughput. Mixed totals are rejected.

rationale.source: GitHub copilot-impact studies 2024; faion-network internal AI-flood audit

rule.rationale: AI commits can flood the repo with micro-PRs that inflate DF and Throughput without raising Earned Value. Splitting preserves comparability across quarters.
